## [00:00 - 03:05]

Alright, so custom conversions are very very different from standard events in the sense that you don't need to access the back end of your client's website in order to track conversions. As long as they have the base pixel across all the pages on their website which they should have anyways, right? That's a given. You should have that anyways. So custom conversions are slightly easier to set up so they give you a lot more flexibility, they take away a lot of the headaches. Now they operate a little bit differently as well. So in the previous example where I was showing you standard events, I'm using the same landing page here just to keep it really really easy. What you'll see here is that instead of going into the code like I did before, so with the standard event I accessed the header code and I installed the standard event as part of the code. With a custom conversion what I'm actually going to do is pull up the URL of the thank you page that follows this. So let's say we had been tracking this page, this Fiji page before and the example in the standard event was Fiji, lead Fiji that we were using. What we need for a custom conversion to do essentially the same exact thing is we would just pull up the URL. Now one of the things you want to be very careful with custom conversions is that this changes. So do not click on this and do not preview because the links are different here. What you want to do is click on this and then you'll essentially copy this URL. You're going to go into your ad account, you're going to select custom conversions, create custom conversion and if it's a URL that has a unique string so you can even just say contains Fiji, so anything with Fiji or you could go equals so it's an exact match to this URL. So if it's exactly this URL you would just do this. If it's a confirmation page, so for example in e-commerce this may happen quite a bit, a confirmation page of their order and the URL is always different, maybe the one word that is constant is confirmation or purchase confirmation. So find out what that is and you would go contains. But if you've got the exact URL and it's always the same just use this and then you're going to go whatever that is to you, let's say that's a lead, you're going to give it a name Fiji lead, you can even set a parameter for it so conversion value and you're going to select

## [03:05 - 05:05]

create. Now custom conversions are pretty awesome because they actually show up in your ad account reporting unlike custom events, so standard events show up, custom events do not, you need to create a custom conversion of the custom event. So you would just pull it up here and once you have saved it you'd be able to go, so slow, slow, slow, slow. Once you've saved your custom conversion you'd be able to bring it up and go Fiji lead and it would show up under here. And so you'd pull it up, you could save this as a preset and it would always be in your reporting and it would show you the number of leads you generated from your ads. So this is how to set up a custom conversion, slightly different. Remember guys, in this example I've just used a landing page to show you this, with events you want to make sure that you're adding the events on the pages that you want to track, correct? So if this is a landing page promoting the event but they don't actually, so here's the next part of the funnel where they request an invite and they book into the calendar and then it's confirmation. If I was trying to track the number of people that booked through the calendar I would install the standard event on this landing page, the confirmation one, and I would also create a custom conversion from this landing page, whatever I was using to track either or. So remember that you want to have tracking along each step of your funnel, but if you're only tracking purchases or conversions or anything like that it's the page that identifies someone having gone through the funnel, usually that's the confirmation page. So that is custom events, standard events, custom conversions, and tracking conversions with the pixel. In the next video I'm going to show you how to do offline tracking conversions.
